channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Trump’s China tariffs spark viral TikTok work-arounds
item.description: President Trump’s trade wars have officially landed on TikTok. U.S. TikTok users’ For You Pages are being flooded with videos from Chinese manufacturers urging Americans to bypass sweeping new tariffs by purchasing goods directly from China.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Docusign Unveils AI-Powered Intelligent Agreement Management Platform
item.description: Docusign, a leading provider of digital signature solutions, has announced the launch of its intelligent agreement management (IAM) platform, powered by artificial intelligence and machine learning algorithms.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: The cybersecurity law that quietly underpins U.S. digital defenses is about to expire
item.description: Nearly a decade after Congress passed the Cybersecurity Information Sharing Act of 2015, the law is facing an uncertain future.
item.dc:creator: Max Ufberg, Fast Company

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Nvidia announces plans to make AI supercomputers in US
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Toshiba's wireless portable turntable is the Sound Burger of 2025 – just don't take it to beach parties
item.description: Toshiba's unveiled a new portable vinyl record player set to be shipped this month, but while it looks like a lot of fun, serious vinyl collectors may be a little wary.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: Tech shares fall after Nvidia says new US controls on exports of AI chip will cost it $5.5 billion
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Shares in computer chipmakers slumped Wednesday after Nvidia said tighter U.S. government controls on exports of computer chips used for artificial intelligence will cost it an extra $5.5 billion.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Rise of Upstage: South Korean AI Startup Expands Globally with Enterprise-Focused Solutions
item.description: South Korean AI startup Upstage is expanding globally, focusing on enterprise clients in the US and Japan with its full-stack AI platform.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Nintendo Switch 2 pre-orders will reportedly begin soon in Canada, and I'm hoping that the US follows suit
item.description: A major leaker claims that Nintendo Switch 2 pre-orders will start soon in Canada but the US date is still up in the air.
item.dc:creator: dash.wood@futurenet.com (Dashiell Wood), TechRadar

item.title: Tech shares fall after Nvidia says new US controls on exports of AI chip will cost it $5.5 billion
item.description: Shares in Nvidia and other tech companies slumped after the computer chipmaker said that tighter U.S. government controls on its exports of semiconductors used for artificial intelligence will cost it an extra $5.5 billion.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Don't like a columnist's opinion? Los Angeles Times offers an AI-generated opposing viewpoint
item.description: In a colorful commentary for the Los Angeles Times, Matt K. Lewis argued that callousness is a central feature of the second Trump administration, particularly its policies of deportation and bureaucratic cutbacks.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Justice Department prohibits employees from sharing anything related to their work on social media
item.description: President Donald Trump’s administration has ordered U.S. Justice Department employees not to post anything on social media related to their government work, after a wave of new political appointees took to cheering Trump and castigating his opponents online. The directive, which was emailed to U.S.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Major U.S. banks pause data sharing with this federal bureau after a cyberattack exposed sensitive information
item.description: Several of the largest U.S. banks are reportedly pausing or reassessing how they send sensitive information to the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) following a major cyberattack on the regulator.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: DHS Cancels Contract Focusing on Neurodiverse Workforce
item.description: The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency has cancelled a contract aimed at improving the recruitment and retention of people with autism and other neurodivergent conditions.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: New survey finds TSA PreCheck membership reduces stress, not time
item.description: PreCheck promises “shorter wait times of under 10 minutes, greater checkpoint efficiency and improved security.
item.dc:creator: Tribune News Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Legacy broadcasters account for just more than 10 percent of TV viewership in March
item.description: Legacy media companies amassed only 10 percent of total television viewership during the month of March, according to new figures from Nielsen Media Research. Nielsen's monthly TV viewership report noted Disney, Paramount, Warner Bros.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: Nvidia wants to build Nvidia AI supercomputers entirely in the US, but I'm not sure it matters so much
item.description: Nvidia says it will build its AI supercomputers entirely in the US - the White House says this is the "Trump Effect.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Fewer Americans want government, tech companies restricting false content online: Poll
item.description: Support for the U.S. government or technology companies' attempts to restrict false information online dropped slightly among Americans over the past two years, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Take a peek at what AMD's next generation Venice Zen 6 EPYC CPU CCD looks like as CEO shows its die
item.description: AMD unveils its next-gen Zen 6 EPYC Venice CPU die, the first HPC chip built on TSMC’s 2nm process node.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Apple’s big plan for better AI is you
item.description: Apple has a brand new plan to make Apple Intelligence smarter. That plan is you — more specifically, your data. A post on the company’s Machine Learning blog explains the plan to begin on-device analysis of your user data.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: About the Best Places to Work in IT
item.description: Nominations for Computerworld’s 2026 Best Places to Work in IT program are now open! Click here to nominate a company today.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: China pursuing 3 alleged US operatives over cyberattacks during Asian Games
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China said Tuesday it is pursuing three alleged U.S. operatives accused of carrying out cyberattacks on Chinese infrastructure during the Asian Games held in the city of Harbin in February. A notice from the Harbin police headquarters named them as Katheryn A. Wilson, Robert J.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: CIOs are overspending on the cloud
item.description: Major unanticipated increases in cloud spending, driven largely by increased use, aren’t spurring CIOs to rethink IT strategies beyond tactical shifts in how they attempt to rein in costs in the cloud.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft releases out-of-band updates to fix reporting error
item.description: Microsoft has released emergency patches to fix an apparent reporting error in Active Directory (AD) Group Policy, which allows administrators to manage and configure user and computer settings in Windows.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg takes the stand in historic antitrust trial
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg took to the witness stand on the first day of a historic antitrust trial to defend his company against allegations it illegally monopolized the social media market.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Intel sells majority stake in Altera to Silver Lake investors in a deal valued at $8.75 billion
item.description: Intel announced Monday that it was selling the majority of its Altera chip business to technology investor Silver Lake in a deal valued at $8.75 billion.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Nvidia plans to manufacture AI chips in the US for the first time
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Nvidia announced Monday that it will produce its artificial intelligence supercomputers in the United States for the first time.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lawmakers call on National Archives to investigate use of Signal and Gmail by top government officials
item.description: Democrats on the Senate Homeland Security Committee say the reported use of Signal and Gmail by national security officials creates major cybersecurity risks.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: One day, you’ll fold your Mac and pop it in your pocket
item.description: iPads are about to become more productive, at least, that’s if the almost seer-like speculation is correct. Apple plans to give its “What’s a PC?” tablets even more Mac-like attributes this year, with details about its plans expected at WWDC 2025 in June.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Meta says it will resume AI training with public content from European users
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Social media company Meta said Monday that it will start using publicly available content from European users to train its artificial intelligence models, resuming work put on hold last year after activists raised concerns about data privacy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: As legacy of Ford fades in Brazil, China's BYD looks to extend global lead in EV sales
item.description: CAMACARI, Brazil (AP) — When Chinese automaker BYD Co. announced plans to build a massive factory in the hardscrabble city of Camacari, in Brazil’s northeastern Bahia state, locals saw a new beginning. After years of economic stagnation following Ford Motor Co.
item.dc:creator: FABIANO MAISONNAVE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Via porn, gore and ultra-violence, extremist groups are sinking hooks online into the very young
item.description: PARIS (AP) — After his arrest, the boy's mother was stunned to discover that her 12-year-old had been learning how to kill and gorging on videos of decapitation and torture so gruesome they made even case-hardened French court officials look away.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hackers find a way around built-in Windows protections
item.description: If not properly managed, Windows Defender Application Control, a security feature with Windows, could be a potential security vulnerability.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: More and faster: New proposals changing Python from within
item.description: Top picks for Python readers on InfoWorld Making Python faster won’t be easy, but it’ll be worth it Python’s malleability and dynamism make it powerful, but also very hard to speed up. Here’s a look at some of the ways developers are working to make Python faster from within.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Arm backs big data center ambitions with migration tools
item.description: Arm Holdings expects its architecture will account for half of the data center CPU market share by the end of this year, and to help achieve those goals it has launched a batch of new migration tools for application porting.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google unveils Firebase Studio for AI app development
item.description: Google is previewing Firebase Studio, a cloud-based agentic development environment designed to build, test, deploy, and run AI applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: OpenAI countersues Elon Musk in legal dispute over ChatGPT maker's business ambitions
item.description: OpenAI is suing Elon Musk for unfair competition and interfering with its business relationships with investors and customers, escalating a legal battle between the ChatGPT maker and the billionaire who helped bankroll the artificial intelligence startup a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
